advantages: Knowing limitations upfront prevents over-trusting fluent but wrong outputs in high-stakes workflows.
Explicit grounding and tool use mitigate hallucination and knowledge gaps without abandoning LLM assistance.
Human-in-the-loop review catches policy false refusals and factual errors before customers see them.
Understanding context limits encourages chunking and summarization strategies that improve reliability.
disadvantages: Mitigations add engineering and process overhead—RAG pipelines, evals, and reviewer time are not free.
Over-correction toward verification can slow teams that treat Claude as infallible autocomplete.
Safety refusals and hedging can frustrate power users even when limitations are understood.
No mitigation removes all risk—adversarial prompts and edge-case hallucinations still require monitoring.
